CITY OF R.ICI{I\4ONW 1,,
City Hall Room 330
2600 Barrett Avenue. Post Ott-ice Box 4046
Richnrond. California 94804
(510) 620-6_509 . FAX (5r0) 620-6518
October 23, L992
Mr. Scott Hallabrin, General Counsel
Fair Political- Practices Commissi-on
428 'J* Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Dear Mr. Hal-Iabrin:
On behalf of Richmond City Council-member LaVonne Niccolls,
hereby request an opinion from the Fair Political Practices
Commission concerning whether and under what circumstances City
Councilmember Niccolls will have a conflict of interest based
upon the following facts:
The spouse of City Councilmember Niccolls is the
trustee of two trusts, one of which is revocable (at
the discretion of the beneficiary) and the other is
irrevocable. 
The beneficiary of the trusts is the
spouse's mother, and upon her death the t.rust proceeds
will go the spouse and the spouse,s brother. 
The trust
contains stocks of numerous companies, some of which
are Fortune 500 companies, and the City Councilmember, s
spouse has the authority to manage the assets of the
trusts. 
Currently, the spouse receives no income from
the trusts other than a fee for administering the
trusts of approximately $2000 annually per trust.
If you need any additional information, please caII me.
Very truly
t
Malcolm Hunter
City Attorney
cc: LaVonne Niccolls
Ri-chmond City Councilmember
Office of the Cit.v Attorney

]\,I,{LCOI-M HUNTER
City Attorney
yours,rilk

sections.question: whether and under what circumstances City Councilmember Niccolls will have a conflict of interest based upon the following facts: The spouse of City Councilmember Niccolls is the trustee of two trusts, one of which is revocable (at the discretion of the beneficiary) and the other is irrevocable. The beneficiary of the trusts is the spouse's mother, and upon her death the trust proceeds will go the spouse and the spouse's brother. The trust contains stocks of numerous companies, some of which are Fortune 500 companies, and the City Councilmember's spouse has the authority to manage the assets of the trusts. Currently, the spouse receives no income from the trusts other than a fee for administering the trusts of approximately $2000 annually per trust.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether City Councilmember Niccolls has a conflict of interest when her spouse serves as trustee of two trusts containing diverse corporate stock holdings and receives trustee administration fees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice is provided in this document. The question section contains detailed factual circumstances about trust assets and trustee responsibilities.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: whether and under what circumstances City Councilmember Niccolls will have a conflict of interest based upon the following facts: The spouse of City Councilmember Niccolls is the trustee of two trusts, one of which is revocable (at the discretion of the beneficiary) and the other is irrevocable. The beneficiary of the trusts is the spouse's mother, and upon her death the trust proceeds will go the spouse and the spouse's brother. The trust contains stocks of numerous companies, some of which are Fortune 500 companies, and the City Councilmember's spouse has the authority to manage the assets of the trusts. Currently, the spouse receives no income from the trusts other than a fee for administering the trusts of approximately $2000 annually per trust.

embedding.summary: City Attorney Malcolm Hunter requests an FPPC opinion on behalf of Richmond City Councilmember LaVonne Niccolls regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from her spouse's role as trustee of two trusts with corporate stock holdings.
